title: 12.-Contracts: On-Call Waterfront Engineering, Design, Environmental Permitting, and Construction Administration Services for Capital Improvement Projects at the Berkeley Waterfront

sections.recommendation:
Adopt five Resolutions authorizing the City Manager to execute contracts and any
amendments with the following firms for on-call waterfront engineering, design,
environmental permitting and construction administration services for capital
improvement projects at the Berkeley Waterfront, for contract periods of December 1,
2025 through November 30, 2028:
1. COWI North America, Inc., for an amount not to exceed $1,000,000.
2. Foth & Van Dyke and Associates, Inc., for an amount not to exceed $1,000,000.
3. GFT Infrastructure, Inc., for an amount not to exceed $1,000,000.
4. GHD Inc., for an amount not to exceed $1,000,000.
5. Moffatt & Nichol, for an amount not to exceed $1,000,000.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
Initial funding for the consultant contracts will be recommended for appropriation
through the FY 2026 first amendment to the appropriations ordinance in the General
Fund (Fund 011), Marina Fund (Fund 608), and State Coastal Conservancy Grant
Fund (Fund 336).
Funding for these contracts are subject to appropriation in future fiscal years based
on the department’s needs for waterfront engineering, design, environmental
permitting and construction administration services.
sections.current_situation:
Over the next several years, the Waterfront Capital Projects Division of the Parks,
Recreation & Waterfront Department is expecting significantly increased workloads,
resulting from deferred maintenance, ongoing capital projects, and outside grants.
The On-Call Waterfront consultants possess the specialized technical, permitting
and environmental compliance expertise needed for waterfront capital projects.
2180 Milvia Street, Berkeley, CA 94704 ● Tel: (510) 981-7000 ● TDD: (510) 981-6903 ● Fax: (510) 981-7099
E-Mail: manager@berkeleyca.gov Website: https://berkeleyca.gov/
Page 2 of 7
Contracts for On-Call CIP Services for Waterfront Projects CONSENT CALENDAR
October 28, 2025
The City has used on-call consultants in the past to design and manage projects to
supplement City staff. These include situations where the nature of the work is
specialized such as landscape architectural design, architectural design, civil
engineering design, electrical design, mechanical design, structural engineering,
permitting, construction administration, inspections, and commissioning of systems. On-
call consultants have also enabled the City to meet tight schedules when short-term
work needs to be addressed. On-call contracts will provide adequate design and
consulting services when needed and not expose the City to the long-term burden of
hiring additional staff and creating significant overhead.
sections.background:
Requests for Qualifications (RFQ) were issued on June 11, 2025 seeking qualified firms
or individuals to provide on-call waterfront engineering, design, environmental permitting
and construction administration services for capital improvement projects. On July 29,
2025, the City received seven proposals from professional consultant firms. A panel of
City staff reviewed the proposals and reviewed, evaluated, and ranked the firms
according to the criteria in the RFQ. After evaluation by the review panel, COWI North
America, Inc., Foth & Van Dyke and Associates, Inc., GFT Infrastructure, Inc., GHD Inc.,
and Moffatt & Nichol were selected as the best qualified to provide these services.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
There are no anticipated negative environmental effects of this action. The execution of
these contracts will help ensure successful completion of several ongoing waterfront
capital improvement projects that will incorporate the sourcing of sustainable and
renewable resources used in construction projects, as well as complying to all regulatory
agency requirements such as Bay Conservation Development Commission, Fish and
Wildlife, Army Corp of Engineers, and Regional Water Control Board. This promotes
environmental sustainability and meets the Strategic Plan goal of providing state-of-the-
art, well-maintained infrastructure, amenities, and facilities.
sections.rationale:
The teams assembled by COWI North America, Inc., Foth & Van Dyke and Associates,
Inc., GFT Infrastructure, Inc., GHD Inc., and Moffatt & Nichol are highly qualified. They
have particular expertise in fulfilling the criteria stated in the Request for Qualifications.
sections.alternatives:
None.
CONTACT PERSON
Scott Ferris, Director, Parks, Recreation & Waterfront, 981-6711
Nelson Lam, Supervising Civil Engineer, Parks, Recreation & Waterfront, 981-6395

RESOLUTION NO.:-N.S.
CONTRACT: COWI NORTH AMERICA, INC. FOR ON-CALL WATERFRONT
ENGINEERING, DESIGN, ENVIRONMENTAL PERMITTING AND CONSTRUCTION
ADMINISTRATION SERVICES FOR CAPITAL IMPROVEMENT PROJECTS
WHEREAS, on June 11, 2025, the City released a Request for Qualifications
(Specification No. 25-11753-C) seeking firms or individuals to provide on-call waterfront
engineering, design, environmental permitting and construction administration services
for capital improvement projects at the Berkeley Waterfront; and
WHEREAS, on July 29, 2025, the City received seven submissions, which were
reviewed and ranked by a panel according to the criteria in the Request for
Qualifications; and
WHEREAS, based on the results of the rating of the submission, COWI North America,
Inc. has been found by has been found to be fully qualified and responsive to the
City’s request; and
WHEREAS, funding will be identified and requested for appropriation as projects arise.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
City Manager is authorized to approve a contract and any amendments with COWI North
America, Inc. for on-call waterfront engineering, design, environmental permitting and
construction administration services for capital improvement projects for the contract
period of December 1, 2025 through November 30, 2028 in an amount not to exceed
$1,000,000. A record signature copy of said contract and any amendments to be on file
in the Office of the City Clerk.
